departments.name: HR & General Affairs

dailyCheckins.content:
# HR & GA — Daily Check-in — 2026-07-05

**Progress:** Resumed the claude.ai handoff session in Claude Code with
real repo access. Reorganized `data-inbox/hr-ga` into the dated-drop
convention, moved SOP and contract templates to `work-products/`, rebuilt
the human org chart from actual deck images (resolved 3 of 4 open gaps),
corrected and re-saved the human org SOW/KPI draft, updated
`org/kpi-dashboard.md` with OD process status, and did a Tier 1 pass on
the SOP and contract templates.
**Blockers:** None.
**Needs Barry today:** 4 Tier 2 items logged to `org/escalation-log.md`,
correspondence backlog of unsigned contracts, 3 open human-org decisions
(Operations Lead ambiguity, Maura's replacement, Agnes's new scope), and
the PKWT template's leftover CO-CEO/shareholder terms.

departments.lessonsLearned:
# HR & GA — Lessons Learned

Append-only log. Use `templates/lessons-learned.template.md` for each
entry. Newest entries first. Consolidate durable lessons into
`playbook.md` monthly.

---

<!-- Add entries below -->

# Lesson — HR & GA — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** A claude.ai session (no repo write access at the time)
reviewed HR data and produced a handoff markdown file plus a draft xlsx,
to be picked up by a Claude Code session with real repo access. The
claude.ai session inferred org reporting lines and annotation meanings
from PDF/deck text extraction alone, without rendering the actual chart
images, and flagged 4 gaps as unresolved guesses.
**Source:** Self-observed, resuming this handoff in Claude Code with git
and `pdftoppm`/image-reading access.
**Lesson:** Text extraction from a slide deck does not reliably preserve
visual layout, an org chart's reporting lines and annotation placement
live in the shapes/lines, not the text run order. Rendering the actual
page as an image (`pdftoppm` + Read, `brew install poppler` if missing)
and reading it directly resolved 3 of the 4 "gaps needing human
confirmation" in minutes, versus guessing from linearized text. The
handoff-file pattern itself worked well: a single markdown file with a
numbered todo list, explicit "action for Claude Code session" callouts,
and a clear list of what's genuinely unresolved vs. inferred, made resuming
in a different session/tool with no shared conversation memory
straightforward.
**Applies to:** Any future cross-session handoff (claude.ai without repo
access to Claude Code with it), and any task involving reading org charts,
diagrams, or other spatially-laid-out content from a deck or PDF, render
the page as an image before trusting extracted text for structure.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — HR & GA — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** Resuming this handoff, found Finance's Claude Code session
was concurrently active in the same repo with its own uncommitted changes
(modified `org/escalation-log.md`, `finance/staff-logs/*`, untracked
`data-inbox/finance/2026-*/` files).
**Source:** Self-observed.
**Lesson:** Multiple department Heads now have simultaneous real git
access to the same repo. Before staging/committing, check `git status` and
`git diff` for unstaged changes in shared files (`org/escalation-log.md`,
`org/kpi-dashboard.md`, `org/org-change-log.md`) that belong to another
department's in-progress work, and never `git add -A`/`git add .`, stage
only the files actually touched for this task so another session's
uncommitted work isn't swept into an unrelated commit.
**Applies to:** Every future session in this repo, now that multiple
Heads write concurrently, this is a standing concern, not a one-off.
**Status:** Logged

departments.name: Finance, Accounting & Tax

dailyCheckins.content:
### 2026-07-05
- Progress: Finalized COA restructure across five rounds with Barry. Full old-to-new mapping confirmed, COGS group created, one account retired (5206), two new accounts added (2208, proposed 1105), CAC tag taxonomy defined (CAC/Non-CAC, then Marketing/Sales). Applied to repo: new COA filed at `finance/reference/chart-of-accounts.csv`, FY2025 journal export split into `data-inbox/finance/2025-01/` through `2025-12/`, FY2025 example statements filed at `finance/reference/`.
- Blockers: Three retroactive cleanups (COGS split, 5309/8201 split, CAC tagging) all need the complete historical journal export. Xendit.csv has data-quality issues (VALUE-error rows). Possible FY2024 books not yet scoped.
- Next: Confirm 1105 with Tax & Compliance, get clean Xendit export, get Barry's scoping decision on FY2024, build CAC tag taxonomy into the journal template with FP&A.

departments.lessonsLearned:
# Finance — Lessons Learned

Append-only log. Use `templates/lessons-learned.template.md` for each
entry. Newest entries first. Consolidate durable lessons into
`playbook.md` monthly.

---

<!-- Add entries below -->

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** Retroactive reclass and full historical CAC tagging approved by Barry
**Source:** Barry feedback
**Lesson:** Retroactive cleanups need the complete data export up front, sampled data (via search) is enough to design the mapping but not to execute it. Should ask for the full export as soon as a retroactive scope is even possible, rather than after approval.
**Applies to:** Any future retroactive reclass or historical data cleanup request.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** COGS/OpEx reclassification and CAC tagging requests surfaced during COA work
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** A "simple" chart-of-accounts cleanup can surface real cross-functional policy decisions (gross margin definition, CAC attribution methodology) that go beyond Finance's own call. Flagging these early and routing to FP&A/Tax & Compliance rather than deciding unilaterally kept the process honest. Barry confirmed that when cross-department clarification is needed (not just cross-work-stream within Finance), the relevant documents should be updated to formally ask the other Head, rather than assuming or deciding on their behalf.
**Applies to:** Any COA or reporting-structure change, always scan for downstream KPI/attribution implications before finalizing, and route genuinely cross-department questions to the relevant Head rather than deciding internally.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** Reviewing a proposed COA restructure line by line
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** A handful of accounts had descriptions that directly contradicted their own account name or excluded the very thing they were named for (5202, 5207, 2207, 5315). These read like copy-paste errors that would have caused real misposting if applied as-is. Barry confirmed this check should also cover MECE (mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive) consistency across different accounts, not just internal name-vs-description consistency.
**Applies to:** Whenever reviewing any proposed COA or template change, check every account's name against its own description AND against every other account's scope for overlap or gaps, before treating it as ready to apply.
**Status:** Logged

# Lesson — Finance — 2026-07-05

**Trigger:** New COA.csv and Example-*.csv files reviewed in data-inbox/finance/
**Source:** Self-observed
**Lesson:** Structural/reference documents (like a chart-of-accounts) sitting in data-inbox/ next to monthly transactional dumps invite confusion about what's live data vs. reference material, and about read-only conventions.
**Applies to:** Any future non-transactional document Barry drops for Finance, route it to finance/reference/ instead of data-inbox/finance/, keep data-inbox strictly for monthly bank/expense/revenue transaction files.
**Status:** Logged

escalations.heading: Escalation - COO Office - 2026-07-05
escalations.issue:
Head of Academic will handle children's learning data. A draft
Student Data Policy now exists at `org/student-data-policy.md` covering
pseudonymization, pre-stripped data-inbox drops, aggregate-first
reporting, a hard ban on student-level data in the public dashboard,
human-delivered family feedback, retention, and incident routing. It
binds all departments and is marked DRAFT until approved.
escalations.recommendation:
Approve the policy as written, or reply with edits.
The one judgment call worth your eyes: rule 1 keeps the
pseudonym-to-identity mapping entirely outside the repo (with you or the
platform database), which means agents can never re-identify students
without you in the loop, safest, but adds a manual step whenever Academic
needs to hand a real intervention list to a human.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
Data-protection policy for children's data is a
company-level commitment, not an operating detail a Head can self-approve.
escalations.neededBy:
Before any real student data lands in
`data-inbox/academic/`.
escalations.crossDept:
All departments, Academic and HR & GA most
directly.
escalations.decision: children's learning data that will be fed into the agents will have no personal data, there will be only just student_id to cross-reference. check if this is already safe. however, if it is not enough, then remember this recommendation and bring it up again when the data is already present. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-06)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — HR & GA — 2026-07-05
escalations.issue:
Auditing `data-inbox/hr-ga/2026-07/correspondence-log.xlsx` (452
real entries) found 38 rows marked `Unsigned`. 16 of those are HR/Legal
contract-type documents (Kontrak, SPK, SPK-FL, SPK-Partnerships, Surat
Kuasa), spanning Aug 2024 to Feb 2025, meaning 5 months to 1.5+ years past
the SOP's own 1-2 business day signing SLA (`hr-ga/work-products/sop-hr-ga.docx`,
Surat/Document workflow, step 3). Several names on the unsigned list belong
to people currently active per the Townhall Q3 2026 org chart (Waitatiri,
CMO; Lana, Creative Lead, appears twice), so this is not obviously stale
data. Full list: HRGA-05 (Waitatiri, SPK), HRGA-11/13/14/19/25 (freelancer
SPKs: Olive Hateem x2, Angga Yulian Saputra x2, Lana Banatul Husna x2),
HRGA-20/21 (Olivyolanda Liani Sianturi, both offer letter and PKWT
contract), HRGA-23/24 (Kontrak, Angga Yulian Saputra), HRGA-26 (Alamii,
partnership SPK), HRGA-01/2025 (Muhamad Gian Giffar), HRGA-07/2025 (Oscar
Muhamad Ramadan), LEGAL-28 (Galih, Surat Kuasa). LEGAL-04 and LEGAL-06
(BNI banking letters) are also unsigned but are GA/banking, not employment
contracts.
escalations.recommendation:
Confirm with Septi whether these are (a) actually
still unsigned, a real compliance gap against the "100% contracts
reviewed/filed pre-execution" KPI, or (b) signed in reality but the tracker
was never updated with the signed-copy link. If (a), prioritize the ones
tied to still-active people (Waitatiri, Lana) first.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
Confirming real-world signature status and, if
genuinely missing, chasing signatures is a real-world action needing a
human who can actually contact the counterparties, not something HR & GA
can resolve from the spreadsheet alone.
escalations.neededBy:
Not time-critical for the older ones, but flag
before treating "contracts reviewed pre-execution: 100%" as accurate in
any KPI report, current status downgraded to `AT RISK` in
`org/kpi-dashboard.md` pending this confirmation.
escalations.decision: Some are unsigned, but some are already signed. Septi will be collecting the already-signed documents, but backdate is the least priority here. Only make sure signed-copies are present for future documents. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-06)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — HR & GA — 2026-07-05
escalations.issue:
Rebuilding the human org chart from the actual Townhall Q3 2026
deck images (`hr-ga/work-products/human-org-chart.md`) resolved 3 of the 4
open gaps from the prior claude.ai session's draft, but surfaced 3 real
open items that need a person, not inference: (1) the deck has two
different versions of the org chart, one with a vacant Operations Lead
role between Barry and Septi/Fira, one without, unclear which is current;
(2) Maura (Learning & Curriculum Specialist) is flagged "to be replaced"
in the deck with no replacement named; (3) Agnes (Community &
Partnerships) is flagged "change roles" with no new scope specified.
escalations.recommendation:
Confirm all three with Septi or Barry before
`hr-ga/work-products/human-org-sow-kpi-draft.xlsx` is finalized or promoted
to a deliverable. Do not guess Maura's replacement or Agnes's new scope.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
These are real personnel/org-design decisions
(who replaces Maura, what Agnes's new role is, whether Operations Lead is
being created), not extractable from the source deck by inference.
escalations.neededBy:
Before the SOW/KPI workbook is promoted from
work-product to deliverable.
escalations.decision: (1) Use the org chart version with the vacant Operations Lead role. (2) Maura is going to be replaced by Siti, Maura's last day is end of July 2026. (3) Agnes takes responsibility for Community & Partnerships (previously Community only) and starts reporting to the CMO from W2 July. To be announced at the townhall next Wednesday. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-05; restored full text after a truncation bug in apply-decision.mjs)_

escalations.heading: Escalation — HR & GA — 2026-07-05
escalations.issue:
Tier 1 review of `hr-ga/work-products/templates/kontrak-pkwt-template.docx`
found it is not a genericized template, it is a lightly-redacted copy of a
real contract (dated 22 Jul 2024, role "CO-CEO", 5-month term, and a
shareholder-specific share-return clause in Pasal 5) matching the
correspondence log's `HRGA - 03/PKWT/VII/2024` entry (Barry Mikhael Cavin).
Reused as-is for a new, non-shareholder hire, the role/date/shareholder
terms would carry over incorrectly. Full detail in
`hr-ga/work-products/tier1-review-sop-and-contracts.md`.
escalations.recommendation:
Genericize the role and date fields, and move the
shareholder share-return clause into a standalone addendum used only when
a hire is actually a shareholder.
escalations.whyNotTier1:
Rewriting the substance of a legal contract
template is a compliance-relevant content decision, not routine
monitoring, flagging rather than silently editing it.
escalations.neededBy: Before this template is next used for a real hire.
escalations.decision: Please hire a legal & compliance sub-agent. Ask it to review the document, then genericize the role and date fields, and move the shareholder share-return clause into a standalone addendum used only when a hire is actually a shareholder. _(via dashboard, 2026-07-05)_
escalations.delegatedTo: n/a (drafting fix stays with HR & GA once approved)

escalations.heading: Escalation — Finance — 2026-07-05
escalations.issue: FY2025 journal entries show PPh21/PPh23 payments made in April 2025 for tax periods back to July 2024, implying company activity predates the earliest journal on file. If FY2024 transactions exist, Finance has no journal or financial statements for that year.
escalations.recommendation: Treat as a Tier 2 item. Do not start building FY2024 books without an explicit go-ahead on scope, this needs Barry to decide timeline and resourcing (in-house vs. external bookkeeping help) before any reconstruction work begins.
escalations.whyNotTier1: Reconstructing a prior fiscal year's books is a resourcing and scope decision, not routine bookkeeping, and the underlying data (if it exists) hasn't been located yet.
escalations.neededBy: Not time-critical, but blocks any FY2024 work stream until scoped.
escalations.decision: Approved as Tier 2, 2026-07-05. Confirms this should not be folded quietly into ongoing Accounting Officer work. Priority: low, sequence after FY2025 and FY2026 books are finished. Scope (timeline, in-house vs. external bookkeeping help) still to be decided before any FY2024 reconstruction work begins.
